svg.aria-label: Animated diagram: a read samples the value-log bucket generation, resolves a pointer and sequence from the LSM, reads the record, and trusts the value only when the generation is unchanged and the record's sequence matches; scans do all of this inside one generation-stable bracket, and batch gets validate every record's sequence the same way. GC swaps make the generation odd, so racing reads retry.
svg.text: Value log: reading while GC moves the bytes
Every read is guarded by two checks — an even, unchanged bucket generation, and a record whose sequence still matches the LSM's.
odd = swap epoch (file rename + LSM re-point) · even = stable

g.text: get(k1) first reads the bucket's generation counter. Even (4) means no GC swap is in flight, so the read may proceed.
The LSM resolves k1 to a pointer AND its write sequence: (page 0, segment 3), seq 41.
The record is read through the page's slot table: slot 3 → record offset → bytes.
Trusted: the generation is still 4 (no swap raced us) and the record's stored seq (41) matches the LSM's. Return the value.
Now GC swaps this bucket's file. The generation goes ODD (5) for the whole swap epoch — file rename AND LSM re-point together.
A get that reads an odd generation does not touch the file: the pointer and the file may disagree right now. It simply retries.
Stale-slot case: a read that resolved before the swap finds a record whose seq is not 41 — GC recycled that slot for another write. Discard, retry.
The retry re-resolves k1 — now (page 0, segment 1) in the new file — reads it, and both checks pass.
Pathological churn only: after a few failed attempts, get falls back to a read holding the bucket write lock, which excludes GC entirely.
A scan opens a generation-stable bracket: it reads EVERY bucket's generation counter before doing any work.
One LSM pass returns keys with their pointers (a paginated scan asks for limit + 1, using the extra one as the next cursor).
File handles are captured and values are batch-read INSIDE the bracket. Resolving a pointer outside it would reopen the wrong-file window.
Afterwards every generation counter is re-read: all even and unchanged → the page is committed. If any moved, the whole closure re-runs.
A GC swap landing mid-scan flips a generation → the scan discards its work and retries. Keys that resolved but read back empty are refetched by single get, outside the bracket.
get_multiple resolves ALL keys in one LSM pass, reading each bucket's L1 file once instead of once per key.
Pointers are grouped by value-log bucket, so each bucket's file is touched exactly once.
Each bucket reads its records in parallel with batched preads — about 2 + N syscalls per page instead of 4 per key.
Every record's seq is checked against the LSM's. Matches are returned; mismatched (recycled) slots are refetched through single-key get.

svg.text: A tombstoned or relocated record can never be served: both checks must pass.
